objects.name: Analytic 1793
objects.description: A defender observes an application establishing application-layer network sessions (e.g., HTTP(S), WebSocket, DNS, SMTP/IMAP) with destinations and request patterns that deviate from the enterprise baseline for that app category, especially when sessions occur during background execution or while the device is locked and exhibit beacon-like periodicity, anomalous SNI/Host patterns, or suspicious request/response size symmetry consistent with command polling and tasking over legitimate-looking protocols.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application-layer protocol traffic exhibiting beacon-like periodicity, anomalous session structure, or protocol misuse patterns

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application-layer indicators observable via enterprise network controls (HTTP method, URI path pattern class, TLS SNI, JA3/ALPN when available, DNS qname/type) showing anomalous or low-and-slow command polling behavior

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Framework-based networking usage spikes or uncommon networking stacks observed by agent telemetry (e.g., repeated URLSession/OkHttp-like patterns) without corresponding foreground/user interaction

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Defines acceptable periodicity variance for network communications
Baseline-dependent threshold for anomalous connection rates
Defines anomaly conditions for encoded or structured payload content
